title: narcissus jonquilla subsp. cordubensis
intro: Narcissus jonquilla subsp. cordubensis, also known as the Cordoban jonquil, is a subspecies of the jonquil narcissus plant, characterized by its strong, sweet fragrance and delicate white flowers with a subtle yellow center. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Narcissus jonquilla subsp. cordubensis include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the flowers and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
